title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: bridgr mixed-frequency forecasting package bridge models, MIDAS-style regressions, intermediate specifications estimate within-period weights data. core workflow always : Provide one lower-frequency target series one higher-frequency indicators. Decide missing indicator observations handled indic_predict. Decide indicators aligned target frequency indic_aggregators. Fit target equation mf_model(), inspect summary(), produce target-period forecasts forecast(). vignette walks workflow package’s built-Swiss GDP barometer data.

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: gdp_growth quarterly, baro monthly. mf_model() recognizes frequency mismatch automatically aligns indicator target frequency fitting target equation.

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: default mean aggregator classic bridge-model setup: monthly block completed first, averaged quarterly frequency target equation estimated. fitted object stores aligned data went estimation future target-period regressor path used forecasting.
code: bridge_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2 )  forecast(bridge_model) #> Mixed-frequency forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #>   time       mean  #> 1 2023-01-01 0.875 #> 2 2023-04-01 0.678 tail(bridge_model$estimation_set) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   time       gdp_growth  baro baro_lag1 gdp_growth_lag1 #>   <date>          <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>           <dbl> #> 1 2021-07-01      2.34  112.      125.            2.47  #> 2 2021-10-01      0.411 104.      112.            2.34  #> 3 2022-01-01      0.105  97.4     104.            0.411 #> 4 2022-04-01      1.03   94.3      97.4           0.105 #> 5 2022-07-01      0.255  90.0      94.3           1.03  #> 6 2022-10-01      0.102  90.7      90.0           0.255 bridge_model$forecast_set #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   time        baro baro_lag1 gdp_growth_lag1 #>   <date>     <dbl>     <dbl> <list>          #> 1 2023-01-01  97.4      90.7 <dbl [1]>       #> 2 2023-04-01  99.8      97.4 <dbl [1]>

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: summary() forecast() use stable package-specific layout. base output across bridge, mixed-frequency, direct-alignment specifications. Additional details, optimization summaries uncertainty settings, appended relevant.
code: summary(bridge_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Estimation rows: 73 #> Regressors: baro, baro_lag1, gdp_growth_lag1 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>                 Estimate #> (Intercept)       -6.249 #> baro               0.151 #> baro_lag1         -0.084 #> gdp_growth_lag1    0.012 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>      Frequency Predict    Aggregation #> baro month     auto.arima mean        #> -----------------------------------

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: package also provides built-plotting method fitted bridge models. type = "forecast", shows observed target history together forecast path generated model.
code: plot(bridge_model, type = "forecast")

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: set indic_predict = "direct", bridgr switches indicator forecasting direct alignment based observed complete high-frequency blocks. case, latest complete blocks assigned backward target periods instead forecast forward first, averaged within target period. particularly useful ragged edge want work observed high-frequency information avoid separate indicator forecasting step.
code: direct_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "direct",   h = 1 )  forecast(direct_model) #> Mixed-frequency forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #>   time       mean  #> 1 2023-01-01 0.483

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: default, bridgr returns point forecasts . want uncertainty output, request estimation time se = TRUE , needed, custom simulation full-system bootstrap controls bootstrap.  uncertainty implementation uses HAC standard errors linear bridge equation, Delta-HAC standard errors parametric aggregation weights estimated jointly. default, prediction intervals simulated resampled centered target-equation residuals. also set full_system_bootstrap = TRUE, bridgr instead uses full-system target-period block bootstrap coefficient standard errors prediction intervals, controlled bootstrap = list(N = ..., block_length = ...).
code: uncertainty_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   target_lags = 1,   h = 4,   se = TRUE,   bootstrap = list(N = 40, block_length = NULL) )  forecast(uncertainty_model) #> Mixed-frequency forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 4 #> Uncertainty: prediction intervals from residual resampling #> Simulation paths: 40 #> ----------------------------------- #>   time       mean  se    lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #> 1 2023-01-01 0.259 0.746 -0.850   1.230    -1.069   1.638    #> 2 2023-04-01 0.486 1.097 -0.433   1.761    -2.025   3.268    #> 3 2023-07-01 0.500 0.827 -0.441   1.370    -1.174   2.638    #> 4 2023-10-01 0.521 0.884 -0.532   1.731    -1.030   2.191 summary(uncertainty_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 4 #> Estimation rows: 74 #> Regressors: baro, gdp_growth_lag1 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>                 Estimate HAC SE #> (Intercept)      -10.988  3.304 #> baro               0.116  0.033 #> gdp_growth_lag1   -0.316  0.120 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>      Frequency Predict    Aggregation #> baro month     auto.arima mean        #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Coefficient SEs: hac #> Prediction intervals: residual resampling #> Simulation paths: 40 #> ----------------------------------- plot(uncertainty_model, type = "forecast")

title: Getting Started with bridgr
text: vignette vignette("mixed-frequency-modeling", package = "bridgr") compares main aggregation strategies shows bridgr moves classic bridge models unrestricted parametric MIDAS-style specifications. vignette vignette("ragged-edge-nowcasting", package = "bridgr") focuses indic_predict different ways handle incomplete high-frequency data forecast origin. vignette vignette("uncertainty--scenarios", package = "bridgr") shows work HAC / Delta-HAC coefficient uncertainty, residual-resampling prediction intervals, optional full-system bootstrap, scenario forecasts based custom future regressor paths.

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: bridgr supports several ways map higher-frequency observations lower-frequency target equation. choices span continuum: Bridge-style deterministic aggregation: "mean", "last", "sum", fixed numeric weights. Unrestricted mixed-frequency regression: "unrestricted". Parametric MIDAS-style weighting: "expalmon" "beta". vignette uses simple monthly--quarterly simulation differences approaches easy see.

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: target intentionally driven middle month quarter, can see different aggregation schemes react. monthly indicator also slot-specific within-quarter movements, unrestricted specification can estimate three distinct monthly coefficients.
code: n_quarters <- 40 quarter_index <- rep(seq_len(n_quarters), each = 3) slot <- rep(1:3, times = n_quarters) monthly_time <- seq(   as.Date("2010-01-01"),   by = "month",   length.out = n_quarters * 3 ) monthly_indicator <- dplyr::tibble(   time = monthly_time,   value = 15 + quarter_index * 0.35 +     ifelse(slot == 1, 0.8 * sin(quarter_index / 2), 0) +     ifelse(slot == 2, -0.6 * cos(quarter_index / 3), 0) +     ifelse(slot == 3, 0.7 * sin(quarter_index / 4 + 0.3), 0) )  quarter_time <- monthly_time[seq(1, length(monthly_time), by = 3)] quarter_target <- dplyr::tibble(   time = quarter_time,   value = 0.5 +     vapply(       seq_along(quarter_time),       function(i) {         block <- monthly_indicator$value[((i - 1) * 3 + 1):(i * 3)]         0.2 * block[[1]] + 0.6 * block[[2]] + 0.2 * block[[3]]       },       numeric(1)     ) +     rep(c(0.1, -0.05, 0.08, -0.02), length.out = length(quarter_time)) )

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: classic bridge-model choices. easy interpret often work well want transparent rule within-period aggregation.
code: mean_model <- mf_model(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   h = 1 )  last_model <- mf_model(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "last",   h = 1 )  summary(mean_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: quarter_target #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Estimation rows: 40 #> Regressors: monthly_indicator #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>                   Estimate #> (Intercept)          0.516 #> monthly_indicator    0.999 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>                   Frequency Predict Aggregation #> monthly_indicator month     last    mean        #> ----------------------------------- summary(last_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: quarter_target #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Estimation rows: 40 #> Regressors: monthly_indicator #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>                   Estimate #> (Intercept)          0.590 #> monthly_indicator    0.993 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>                   Frequency Predict Aggregation #> monthly_indicator month     last    last        #> -----------------------------------

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: "unrestricted" estimates one coefficient within-quarter monthly observation. monthly--quarterly example means three separate regressors. example keeps indic_predict = "last" comparison isolates aggregation choice. ragged-edge vignette covers indic_predict = "direct", skips indicator forecasting instead works latest observed complete high-frequency blocks.
code: unrestricted_model <- mf_model(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "unrestricted",   h = 1 )  summary(unrestricted_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: quarter_target #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Estimation rows: 40 #> Regressors: monthly_indicator_hf1, monthly_indicator_hf2, monthly_indicator_hf3 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>                       Estimate #> (Intercept)              0.541 #> monthly_indicator_hf1    0.198 #> monthly_indicator_hf2    0.596 #> monthly_indicator_hf3    0.205 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>                   Frequency Predict Aggregation  #> monthly_indicator month     last    unrestricted #> -----------------------------------

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: bridgr also supports parametric weighting rules estimate within-period shape data keeping number free parameters small. fitted object stores estimated weight profile underlying parametric coefficients.
code: expalmon_model <- mf_model(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 1, maxiter = 100),   h = 1 )  beta_model <- mf_model(   target = quarter_target,   indic = monthly_indicator,   indic_predict = "last",   indic_aggregators = "beta",   solver_options = list(     seed = 123,     n_starts = 1,     maxiter = 100,     start_values = c(2, 2)   ),   h = 1 ) indicator_id <- expalmon_model$indic_name[[1]]  dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::tibble(     model = "expalmon",     month = seq_along(expalmon_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]),     weight = expalmon_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "beta",     month = seq_along(beta_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]),     weight = beta_model$parametric_weights[[indicator_id]]   ) ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   model    month    weight #>   <chr>    <int>     <dbl> #> 1 expalmon     1 1.99 e- 1 #> 2 expalmon     2 5.97 e- 1 #> 3 expalmon     3 2.05 e- 1 #> 4 beta         1 8.88 e-16 #> 5 beta         2 1.000e+ 0 #> 6 beta         3 8.88 e-16

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: models share downstream interface.
code: dplyr::bind_rows(   dplyr::tibble(     model = "mean",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(mean_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "last",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(last_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "unrestricted",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(unrestricted_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "expalmon",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(expalmon_model)$mean)   ),   dplyr::tibble(     model = "beta",     forecast = as.numeric(forecast(beta_model)$mean)   ) ) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   model        forecast #>   <chr>           <dbl> #> 1 mean             29.0 #> 2 last             28.9 #> 3 unrestricted     29.0 #> 4 expalmon         29.0 #> 5 beta             29.0

title: Aggregation and Mixed-Frequency Modeling in bridgr
text: rough guide: Use deterministic bridge aggregation like “mean”, “last”, “sum” want transparent stable nowcasting rule. Use "unrestricted" frequency gap small want within-period observation coefficient. Use "expalmon" "beta" want data-driven within-period weights like parsimonious parameterization "unrestricted". Use indic_predict = "direct" want direct alignment based latest observed complete high-frequency blocks. key design choice bridgr specifications share estimation, forecasting, summary workflow. can therefore move classic bridge models, unrestricted mixed-frequency regressions, parametric MIDAS-style models without switching different API.

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: Mixed-frequency nowcasting usually happens ragged edge: lower-frequency target released yet, higher-frequency indicators partially observed current target period. bridgr handles situation indic_predict, controls missing high-frequency tail completed target equation estimated forecast. currently supported options : "last": extend latest available high-frequency observation. "mean": extend mean latest available high-frequency block. "auto.arima": fit automatic ARIMA model indicator forecast missing observations. "ets": fit ETS model indicator forecast missing observations. "direct": forecast indicator . Instead, align latest observed complete high-frequency blocks directly target periods average within target period.

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: start package’s quarterly GDP growth series monthly barometer, remove last quarterly GDP observation last two monthly indicator observations. creates clean nowcast setup: final quarter now forecasted rather estimated, monthly indicator partially observed forecast quarter. target history now ends one quarter earlier, monthly indicator contains partial block quarter want nowcast.

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: model specification case. difference missing monthly observations completed indicator aggregated quarterly frequency.
code: predict_methods <- c("last", "mean", "auto.arima", "ets")  models <- lapply(   predict_methods,   function(method) {     mf_model(       target = gdp_nowcast,       indic = baro_ragged,       indic_predict = method,       indic_aggregators = "mean",       target_lags = 1,       h = 1     )   } ) names(models) <- predict_methods  dplyr::bind_rows(   lapply(     predict_methods,     function(method) {       fc <- forecast(models[[method]])       dplyr::tibble(         method = method,         forecast_time = fc$time,         forecast = as.numeric(fc$mean)       )     }   ) ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   method     forecast_time forecast #>   <chr>      <date>           <dbl> #> 1 last       2022-10-01      -0.968 #> 2 mean       2022-10-01      -0.876 #> 3 auto.arima 2022-10-01      -0.706 #> 4 ets        2022-10-01      -0.968

what: Looking at the Forecast Regressor Set
title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: easiest way see method inspect forecast_set, stores target-period regressors used final forecast. "last", last observed monthly value extended forward. "mean", mean latest available high-frequency block extended forward instead.
code: lapply(   models[c("last", "mean")],   function(model) {     model$forecast_set   } ) #> $last #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   time       baro_ragged gdp_nowcast_lag1 #>   <date>           <dbl> <list>           #> 1 2022-10-01        87.5 <dbl [1]>        #>  #> $mean #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   time       baro_ragged gdp_nowcast_lag1 #>   <date>           <dbl> <list>           #> 1 2022-10-01        88.2 <dbl [1]>

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: "direct" takes different route. indicator forecasted. Instead, bridgr assigns latest complete high-frequency blocks backward target periods averages within target period. especially useful want avoid separate indicator forecasting step prefer work observed high-frequency data.
code: direct_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_nowcast,   indic = baro_ragged,   indic_predict = "direct",   h = 1 )  forecast(direct_model) #> Mixed-frequency forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_nowcast #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #>   time       mean   #> 1 2022-10-01 -0.150

title: Ragged-Edge Nowcasting with bridgr
text: rough guide: Use "last" simple carry-forward rule acceptable. Use "mean" want stable deterministic fill based latest high-frequency block. Use "auto.arima" "ets" indicator meaningful time-series structure separate extrapolation step sensible. Use "direct" want direct alignment without indicator forecasting want rely latest observed complete high-frequency blocks. good part downstream workflow change. summary() forecast() interface works across nowcasting choices.

bridgr can return point forecasts , can also compute coefficient uncertainty fitted target equation prediction intervals forecasts. relevant estimation arguments : se = FALSE: point forecasts . se = TRUE: compute HAC Delta-HAC coefficient uncertainty prediction intervals. bootstrap = list(N = 100, block_length = NULL): control number predictive simulation paths. block_length used full_system_bootstrap = TRUE. full_system_bootstrap = TRUE: replace default residual-resampling prediction intervals HAC / Delta-HAC coefficient standard errors full-system target-period block bootstrap. refits full bridge workflow every draw, can substantially slower.

what: Fitting a Model with Default Uncertainty
text: bridgr computes HAC standard errors linear bridge equation, Delta-HAC standard errors parametric aggregation weights estimated jointly. Forecast uncertainty obtained simulating resampled centered residuals fitted target equation.
code: gdp_growth <- suppressMessages(tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(gdp)))  boot_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2,   se = TRUE,   bootstrap = list(N = 20, block_length = NULL) )

text: model estimated se = TRUE, forecast() returns standardized forecast object : mean se lower upper forecast_set uncertainty metadata intervals empirical prediction intervals based stored residual- resampling forecast draws.
code: fc <- forecast(boot_model)  fc #> Mixed-frequency forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Uncertainty: prediction intervals from residual resampling #> Simulation paths: 20 #> ----------------------------------- #>   time       mean  se    lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #> 1 2023-01-01 0.875 0.949 -0.430   1.794    -1.461   2.943    #> 2 2023-04-01 0.678 0.671 -0.470   1.030    -1.670   1.225 fc$bootstrap #> $requested #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $enabled #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $N #> [1] 20 #>  #> $valid_N #> [1] 0 #>  #> $block_length #> NULL

text: uncertainty configuration also feeds summary(). printed summary keeps base layout point-estimate model adds uncertainty section uncertainty output available.
code: summary(boot_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Estimation rows: 73 #> Regressors: baro, baro_lag1, gdp_growth_lag1 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>                 Estimate HAC SE #> (Intercept)       -6.249  1.424 #> baro               0.151  0.033 #> baro_lag1         -0.084  0.031 #> gdp_growth_lag1    0.012  0.073 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>      Frequency Predict    Aggregation #> baro month     auto.arima mean        #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Coefficient SEs: hac #> Prediction intervals: residual resampling #> Simulation paths: 20 #> -----------------------------------

text: want forecast target different future regressor path, pass custom xreg object forecast(). custom regressor names must match ones used fitted target equation. Scenario forecasts reuse uncertainty method evaluate supplied regressor path.
code: scenario_xreg <- dplyr::tibble(   id = rep(boot_model$xreg_names, each = nrow(boot_model$forecast_base_set)),   time = rep(     boot_model$forecast_base_set$time,     times = length(boot_model$xreg_names)   ),   value = c(     boot_model$forecast_base_set$baro + 5,     boot_model$forecast_base_set$baro_lag1 + 5   ) )  scenario_fc <- forecast(boot_model, xreg = scenario_xreg)  dplyr::tibble(   time = fc$time,   baseline = as.numeric(fc$mean),   scenario = as.numeric(scenario_fc$mean) ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   time       baseline scenario #>   <date>        <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01    0.875     1.21 #> 2 2023-04-01    0.678     1.02

text: want propagate uncertainty full bridge workflow, including indicator completion aggregation, set full_system_bootstrap = TRUE. can substantially slower default residual-resampling intervals bootstrap draw re-estimates full bridge pipeline. mode, reported coefficient standard errors forecast intervals based bootstrap draws.
code: full_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2,   se = TRUE,   full_system_bootstrap = TRUE,   bootstrap = list(N = 20, block_length = NULL) )  forecast(full_model)$bootstrap #> $requested #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $enabled #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $N #> [1] 20 #>  #> $valid_N #> [1] 20 #>  #> $block_length #> [1] 5

text: interested point estimates, leave se = FALSE. case, bootstrap ignored forecast() still returns object shape, NA uncertainty fields.
code: point_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 1,   se = FALSE,   bootstrap = list(N = 20) )  forecast(point_model) #> Mixed-frequency forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #>   time       mean  #> 1 2023-01-01 0.875

text: default se = TRUE, intervals residual-resampling prediction intervals fitted target equation, coefficient standard errors come HAC Delta-HAC estimation. full_system_bootstrap = TRUE, coefficient standard errors forecast intervals come full-system block bootstrap resamples target-period blocks aligned mixed-frequency system, refits bridge workflow draw, evaluates resulting future paths.

title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Burri M (2026). bridgr: Bridging Data Frequencies Timely Economic Forecasts. R package version 0.1.2, https://github.com/marcburri/bridgr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {bridgr: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts},   author = {Marc Burri},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.2},   url = {https://github.com/marcburri/bridgr}, }

what: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
text: bridgr designed simplify implementation evaluation bridge models, useful nowcasting (predicting present near-term) forecasting macroeconomic variables like GDP. Bridge models statistical tools link high-frequency indicators (e.g., monthly industrial production) low-frequency target variables (e.g., quarterly GDP) forecasting aggregating indicators match target’s frequency. enable timely predictions official release low-frequency data, making essential policymakers need early insights decision-making. bridgr supports regular frequency ladders second year, several deterministic model-based indicator forecasting rules, multiple aggregation choices including joint expalmon beta weighting. target period contains high-frequency observations implied current frequency mapping, package keeps recent observations reports summarized warning.

title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
text: CRAN: can install development version bridgr like :
code: install.packages("bridgr") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("marcburri/bridgr")

title: Bridging Data Frequencies for Timely Economic Forecasts
text: example estimates bridge model uncertainty output: se = TRUE, bridgr reports HAC Delta-HAC coefficient standard errors residual-resampling prediction intervals. want data-driven within-period weights instead simple deterministic aggregator, can switch indicator aggregator expalmon. corresponding weights estimated jointly within bridge model, solver_options let control optimization.
code: suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(bridgr))  gdp <- suppressMessages(tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(bridgr::gdp)))  bridge_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 2,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2,   se = TRUE )  forecast(bridge_model) #> Mixed-frequency forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Uncertainty: prediction intervals from residual resampling #> Simulation paths: 100 #> ----------------------------------- #>   time       mean  se    lower_80 upper_80 lower_95 upper_95 #> 1 2023-01-01 0.808 0.681 -0.191   1.621    -0.757   2.046    #> 2 2023-04-01 0.468 0.771 -0.469   1.300    -1.158   2.763 summary(bridge_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Estimation rows: 72 #> Regressors: baro, baro_lag1, baro_lag2, gdp_lag1 #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>             Estimate HAC SE #> (Intercept)   -6.356  1.254 #> baro           0.159  0.031 #> baro_lag1     -0.134  0.049 #> baro_lag2      0.042  0.019 #> gdp_lag1       0.223  0.143 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>      Frequency Predict    Aggregation #> baro month     auto.arima mean        #> ----------------------------------- #> Uncertainty: #> Coefficient SEs: hac #> Prediction intervals: residual resampling #> Simulation paths: 100 #> ----------------------------------- expalmon_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 3),   h = 1 )  summary(expalmon_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Estimation rows: 75 #> Regressors: baro #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>             Estimate #> (Intercept)   -9.371 #> baro           0.098 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>      Frequency Predict    Aggregation #> baro month     auto.arima expalmon    #> ----------------------------------- #> Estimated parametric aggregation: #> baro weights: 0.006, 0.994, 0.000 #> baro parameters: -4.914, -10.000 #> ----------------------------------- #> Joint parametric aggregation optimization: #> Method: L-BFGS-B #> Objective value: 60.832 #> Convergence code: 0 #> Best start: 1 / 3 #> -----------------------------------

title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
text: baro: KOF barometer, monthly business cycle indicator. fcurve: F-curve, daily business cycle indicator. gdp: Quarterly GDP data (real, seasonally adjusted). wea: Weekly Economic Activity (WEA) indicator.

title: Swiss Economic Indicators — gdp
text: baro: Source: KOF Swiss Economic Institute Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Monthly Format: tibble monthly observations 2 variables: time: Date, month year observation. values: Numeric, value KOF barometer. fcurve: Source: Burri Kaufmann GitHub Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Daily Format: tibble daily observations 2 variables: time: Date, date observation. values: Numeric, value F-curve (inverted compatibility). gdp: Source: SECO Timeframe: January 2004 - December 2022 Frequency: Quarterly Format: tibble quarterly observations 2 variables: time: Date, quarter year observation. values: Numeric, real GDP (seasonally adjusted). wea: Source: SECO WEA Indicator Timeframe: January 2005 - December 2022 Frequency: Weekly Format: tibble weekly observations 2 variables: time: Date, week year observation. values: Numeric, value WEA.

title: Estimate a Mixed-Frequency Model — mf_model
text: Estimate bridge model links one lower-frequency target series one higher-frequency indicator series. Indicators aligned target frequency forecasting missing higher-frequency observations aggregating within target period.

title: Estimate a Mixed-Frequency Model — mf_model
code: mf_model(   target,   indic,   indic_predict = NULL,   indic_aggregators = NULL,   indic_lags = 0,   target_lags = 0,   h = 1,   frequency_conversions = NULL,   se = FALSE,   bootstrap = NULL,   full_system_bootstrap = FALSE,   solver_options = NULL,   ... )  bridge(...)

title: Estimate a Mixed-Frequency Model — mf_model
text: target single target series tsbox::ts_boxable() format. indic One indicator series tsbox::ts_boxable() format. indic_predict character vector indicator forecasting methods. Length must 1 equal number indicator series. Setting indic_predict = "direct" switches direct MIDAS-style alignment: indicators forecasted, recent complete high-frequency blocks assigned backward target periods. Direct alignment must used indicators . h > 1, latest complete block assigned farthest requested forecast horizon earlier complete blocks assigned backward . indic_predict = "mean", missing high-frequency observations filled mean latest available obs_per_target high-frequency observations, mean extended across forecast horizon. indic_aggregators character vector aggregation methods list numeric weights. Length must 1 equal number indicator series. Numeric weights must sum one appropriate length inferred target-period block size. "unrestricted" keeps one separate coefficient per high-frequency observation within target period. parametric aggregators use two coefficients : "expalmon" uses (linear, quadratic), "beta" uses (left_shape, right_shape) normalized beta shape parameters. indic_predict = "direct", indic_aggregators ignored direct blocks averaged within target period. indic_lags non-negative integer giving number target-period lags add aggregated indicator. target_lags non-negative integer giving autoregressive order target equation. h positive integer forecast horizon measured target periods. frequency_conversions named numeric vector used customize regular frequency ladder. Supported names spm, mph, hpd, dpw, wpm, mpq, qpy. se Logical flag indicating whether coefficient standard errors prediction intervals computed. TRUE, mf_model() reports HAC standard errors linear target equation, Delta-HAC standard errors parametric aggregation weights estimated jointly. bootstrap list uncertainty controls. Currently list(N = 100, block_length = NULL) supported. N number predictive simulation paths used se = TRUE. full_system_bootstrap = TRUE, N controls number full-system target-period block-bootstrap replications used prediction intervals. block_length used full-system bootstrap. block_length NULL, mf_model() uses ceiling(n^(1/3)) based final target-period sample size. full_system_bootstrap Logical flag indicating whether prediction intervals coefficient standard errors based full-system target-period block bootstrap instead residual resampling HAC / Delta-HAC uncertainty fitted target equation. option used se = TRUE. refits full bridge workflow every draw, full_system_bootstrap = TRUE can substantially slower default residual-resampling intervals. solver_options list optional controls joint parametric-weight optimization. Supported entries : method optimizer ("L-BFGS-B", "BFGS", "Nelder-Mead", "nlminb"), maxiter iteration budget per optimization run, n_starts number multi-start attempts, seed reproducible random restarts, trace optimizer verbosity, start_values user-supplied initial parameter values. start_values can either numeric vector named list. numeric vector, values concatenated indicator order across parametric aggregators. Within indicator, parameter order (linear, quadratic) "expalmon" (left_shape, right_shape) "beta". Named-list start_values must provide exactly required number values parametric indicator. controls ignored unless least one indicator uses parametric aggregator. ... Reserved future extensions.

title: Estimate a Mixed-Frequency Model — mf_model
text: object class "mf_model" containing standardized input series, inferred frequencies, aligned estimation forecast datasets, fitted target model, fitted indicator models, metadata required forecast.mf_model() summary.mf_model().

title: Estimate a Mixed-Frequency Model — mf_model
text: Supported indicator forecasting methods "mean", "last", "auto.arima", "ets", "direct". Supported aggregation methods "mean", "last", "sum", "unrestricted", "expalmon", "beta", numeric weight vector supplied inside list(). "unrestricted" expands high-frequency observation within target period bridge regressor, corresponds U-MIDAS style specification frequency gap small. one indicators use parametric aggregator, corresponding aggregation weights estimated jointly final bridge-model objective rather one indicator time. Unrestricted mixed-frequency regressions can become parameter-heavy quickly. indic_aggregators = "unrestricted", mf_model() warns final estimation sample contains fewer 10 observations per predictor bridge regression. package assumes regular frequency ladder second -> minute -> hour -> day -> week -> month -> quarter -> year. default number lower-level observations per higher-level unit : spm = 60 mph = 60 hpd = 24 dpw = 7 wpm = 4 mpq = 3 qpy = 4 Users can override subset values frequency_conversions. target period contains high-frequency observations implied current mapping, mf_model() keeps recent observations emits summarized warning. target period contains fewer observations required, call fails. Month-, quarter-, year-based input dates standardized period starts needed frequency recognition.

title: Estimate a Mixed-Frequency Model — mf_model
text: bridge() retained compatibility forwards mf_model() deprecation warning.

title: Estimate a Mixed-Frequency Model — mf_model
text: Baffigi, ., Golinelli, R., & Parigi, G. (2004). Bridge models forecast euro area GDP. International Journal Forecasting, 20(3), 447-460. doi:10.1016/S0169-2070(03)00067-0 Ghysels, E., Sinko, ., & Valkanov, R. (2007). MIDAS regressions: results new directions. Econometric Reviews, 26(1), 53-90. doi:10.1080/07474930600972467 Andreou, E., Ghysels, E., & Kourtellos, . (2010). Regression models mixed sampling frequencies. Journal Econometrics, 158(2), 246-261. doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2010.01.004 Schumacher, C. (2016). comparison MIDAS bridge equations. International Journal Forecasting, 32(2), 257-270. doi:10.1016/j.ijforecast.2015.07.004 Burri, M. (2026). Nowcasting Swiss GDP Growth Public Lead Texts: Simple Methods Sufficient. Oxford Bulletin Economics Statistics, 1-25. doi:10.1111/obes.70073

title: Estimate a Mixed-Frequency Model — mf_model
code: gdp_growth <- suppressMessages(tsbox::ts_na_omit(tsbox::ts_pc(gdp)))  model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "mean",   indic_lags = 1,   target_lags = 1,   h = 2 )  expalmon_model <- mf_model(   target = gdp_growth,   indic = baro,   indic_predict = "auto.arima",   indic_aggregators = "expalmon",   solver_options = list(seed = 123, n_starts = 3),   h = 1 )  forecast(model) #> Mixed-frequency forecast #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Forecast horizon: 2 #> Uncertainty: point forecast only #> ----------------------------------- #>   time       mean  #> 1 2023-01-01 0.875 #> 2 2023-04-01 0.678 summary(expalmon_model) #> Mixed-frequency model summary #> ----------------------------------- #> Target series: gdp_growth #> Target frequency: quarter #> Forecast horizon: 1 #> Estimation rows: 75 #> Regressors: baro #> ----------------------------------- #> Target equation coefficients: #>             Estimate #> (Intercept)   -9.371 #> baro           0.098 #> ----------------------------------- #> Indicator summary: #>      Frequency Predict    Aggregation #> baro month     auto.arima expalmon    #> ----------------------------------- #> Estimated parametric aggregation: #> baro weights: 0.006, 0.994, 0.000 #> baro parameters: -4.914, -10.000 #> ----------------------------------- #> Joint parametric aggregation optimization: #> Method: L-BFGS-B #> Objective value: 60.832 #> Convergence code: 0 #> Best start: 1 / 3 #> -----------------------------------

title: bridgr (development version)
text: Rename main model-construction entry point mf_model(), rename fitted-model class S3 methods mf_model, keep bridge() deprecated compatibility wrapper. Extend mf_model() beyond classic bridge aggregation: add unrestricted mixed-frequency regressors via indic_aggregators = "unrestricted" add parametric "beta" weighting alongside "expalmon" add direct high-frequency alignment via indic_predict = "direct" support fixed numeric aggregation weights supplied list() Improve mixed-frequency input handling: infer regular frequencies second year allow custom frequency_conversions standardize month-, quarter-, year-end dates period starts needed frequency recognition keep recent observations overfilled target periods summarized warning fail target periods contain high-frequency observations Add joint parametric aggregation optimization controls solver_options, including optimizer choice, multi-start runs, seeds, iteration limits, user-supplied starting values. Add uncertainty support: se = TRUE coefficient uncertainty prediction intervals HAC standard errors linear bridge equations Delta-HAC standard errors parametric aggregation weights estimated jointly residual-resampling prediction intervals default optional full-system block bootstrap uncertainty via full_system_bootstrap = TRUE Add scenario forecasting support forecast.mf_model() custom future xreg paths standardized forecast objects uncertainty metadata. Add plotting methods helpers: plot.mf_model() fit forecast plots theme_bridgr(), colors_bridgr(), scale_color_bridgr(), scale_fill_bridgr() Expand printed output documentation: standardize summary.mf_model() forecast.mf_model() output add vignettes mixed-frequency modeling, ragged-edge nowcasting, uncertainty / scenario analysis refresh README examples package references Remove legendre parametric aggregation option. Use analytic gradients expalmon optimization improve normalized beta polynomial gradient used optimizer.

title: bridgr 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-18 Solve dependency issues xts

title: bridgr 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-13 Added gdp,baro, wea fcurve datasets. Added bridge(), forecast() summary() functions. Supports target variables monthly, quarterly yearly frequency, indicator variables daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly yearly frequency. Supports auto.arima, ets methods indicator variable forecasting. Supports aggregation indicator variables match target’s frequency using custom weighting functions, exponential Almon polynomials methods.
